Title: Aquilaria sinensis MYB-Like Transcription Factor AsMYB1 Promotes 2-(2-Phenylethyl)Chromone Biosynthesis Through Interacting With 14-3-3 Family Protein AsGRF1

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes 2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone (PEC) biosynthesis and accumulation as the key PEC skeletal biosynthetic enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsPECPS transcript is strongly induced by salt treatment in A. sinensis calli. 2. AsMYB1 directly binds the AsPECPS promoter and activates its transcription. 3. Knockdown/knockout of regulators AsMYB1 or AsGRF1 reduces AsPECPS expression together with PEC accumulation, supporting AsPECPS as the central biosynthetic output gene for PEC production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome analysis of 24 h and 120 h salt-treated calli; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis under salt stress; regulatory promoter assays (Y1H, dual-luciferase, EMSA, ChIP-qPCR) showing direct activation by AsMYB1; metabolite profiling by Qtrap-MS as downstream readout
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PECs are major agarwood constituents with diverse pharmacological activities and are implicated in scavenging reactive oxygen species under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the pivotal/key gene controlling PEC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AsPECPS promoter; 35S used in reporter/effector assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of the PEC skeleton, which is subsequently diversified by post-modification enzymes such as hydroxylases and O-methyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: A. sinensis calli and suspension cells; agarwood resin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states AsPECPS was previously reported by the authors and serves here mainly as the direct biosynthetic target of AsMYB1/AsGRF1 regulation rather than the newly characterized focal gene.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the core skeleton of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stacking activation of AsPECPS with positive regulators such as AsMYB1 and AsGRF1 may improve stress-induced agarwood quality and PEC yield

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively promotes PEC accumulation under salt stress by directly activating AsPECPS transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsMYB1 is a salt-inducible 1R-subtype MYB-like transcription factor whose expression is highly correlated with AsPECPS. 2. Overexpression of AsMYB1 increases AsPECPS expression and total/major PEC accumulation, whereas RNAi and CRISPR editing strongly reduce both. 3. AsMYB1 directly binds MYB binding sites in the AsPECPS promoter and activates transcription. 4. AsGRF1 interacts with AsMYB1 and further enhances AsMYB1-mediated PEC biosynthesis by increasing its transcriptional activity, nuclear localization, and protein stability.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by 150 mM NaCl; positively correlated with AsPECPS; examined in calli and suspension cells
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; direct promoter binding; activity enhanced by protein interaction-mediated nuclear accumulation and stabilization
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome analysis of 24 h and 120 h salt-treated calli; IP-MS for interacting proteins; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: A. sinensis calli and suspension cells; agarwood resin
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong positive regulator suitable for metabolic engineering to enhance salt-induced PEC production and agarwood quality; may be combined with AsGRF1 and AsPECPS
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PECs are pharmacologically active agarwood constituents and may help scavenge reactive oxygen species under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in suspension cells; RNAi in calli; CRISPR/Cas9 editing; qRT-PCR; Qtrap-MS metabolite profiling; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive upstream activator of stress-induced PEC biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PEC biosynthesis via control of core skeletal biosynthetic gene transcription
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression and reporter effector constructs; native AsPECPS promoter as target
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional complex with 14-3-3 family protein AsGRF1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 1R-subtype MYB-like transcription factor; CCA1-like/R-R-type MYB; SHAQKYF motif
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains three predicted 14-3-3 binding motifs, but deletion of these motifs did not abolish interaction with AsGRF1, suggesting atypical/phosphorylation-independent binding. AsMYB1 overexpression also increased AsOMT1-4 expression, but direct regulation was not demonstrated.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AsPECPS transcription by binding MBS cis-elements in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broader effects on other secondary metabolites are unresolved; discussion notes possible influence beyond PECs

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively facilitates AsMYB1-mediated PEC biosynthesis under salt stress; loss of AsGRF1 reduces AsPECPS expression and PEC accumulation, but AsGRF1 overexpression alone is insufficient to activate PEC production strongly.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsGRF1 is a salt-inducible 14-3-3 family protein whose expression correlates with AsMYB1 and AsPECPS. 2. AsGRF1 physically interacts with AsMYB1 in vivo and in vitro. 3. Knockdown or knockout of AsGRF1 reduces AsPECPS transcript abundance and PEC accumulation under salt stress. 4. Co-overexpression with AsMYB1 enhances AsPECPS expression and PEC accumulation more than AsMYB1 alone, while co-silencing has additive suppressive effects. 5. AsGRF1 increases AsMYB1 DNA-binding/transcriptional activity, nuclear accumulation, and stability by slowing proteasome-mediated degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Salt-inducible in calli; expression highly correlated with AsPECPS and AsMYB1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated coactivation; promotes nuclear localization and stabilization of a TF; enhances DNA binding/transcriptional output
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profiling under salt stress; IP-MS-based candidate interactor screening from N. benthamiana; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: A. sinensis calli and suspension cells; agarwood resin
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a synergistic cofactor to pair with AsMYB1 for engineering enhanced PEC production; not sufficient alone
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PECs are pharmacologically active agarwood constituents and may function in ROS scavenging during salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression, RNAi, CRISPR/Cas9, qRT-PCR, Qtrap-MS metabolite profiling, LCI, BiFC, Co-IP, GST pull-down, ChIP-qPCR enhancement assay, dual-luciferase, localization assay, cell-free degradation assay, MG132/CHX assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive co-regulator required for full AsMYB1 activity in stress-induced PEC production
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation of PEC biosynthesis through TF activity control
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in overexpression and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress / 150 mM NaCl; MG132 and CHX in protein stability assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 14-3-3 family protein (GRF); omega subgroup; nine conserved alpha-helices
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction with AsMYB1 persisted after deletion of predicted 14-3-3-binding motifs in AsMYB1, implying atypical binding. AsGRF1 alone does not markedly activate AsPECPS or PEC accumulation in overexpression lines.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly promotes AsPECPS expression by enhancing AsMYB1 function
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alone showed little gain in PEC output, indicating dependency on AsMYB1 context
